rag.description: The MoveMouseCursor action moves the mouse cursor to a target web element or to a coordinate position on the page. When an element is provided via OnElement it takes precedence over any coordinate arguments and the cursor moves directly to that element. When no element is specified, X and Y parameters define the target position and the optional Origin parameter controls whether the movement is relative to the current pointer position ('Pointer') or to the top-left corner of the visible viewport ('Viewport').

qa.question: What does the MoveMouseCursor action do?
qa.answer: MoveMouseCursor moves the mouse cursor to a target web element when one is specified, or to the given X and Y coordinates on the page when no element is provided.

qa.question: What happens when both OnElement and coordinates are provided?
qa.answer: When OnElement is specified, element targeting takes precedence and the action moves the cursor to that element; X, Y, and Origin are ignored.

qa.question: What does the Origin parameter control?
qa.answer: Origin controls the reference point for the coordinate movement. 'Pointer' moves the cursor relative to its current position; 'Viewport' moves it relative to the top-left corner of the visible viewport. The default is 'pointer'.

qa.question: What alias does MoveMouseCursor accept?
qa.answer: MoveMouseCursor is also accepted as MoveToElement — both resolve to identical behavior.

qa.question: What are the default values for X and Y when not provided?
qa.answer: When X or Y are absent from the argument, they default to 0.

qa.question: What W3C WebDriver endpoint does MoveMouseCursor use?
qa.answer: MoveMouseCursor uses the W3C WebDriver POST /session/{session id}/actions endpoint.

qa.question: What is the manifest version for MoveMouseCursor?
What locator strategies does MoveMouseCursor support when targeting an element?
qa.answer: It supports Xpath (default), CssSelector, Id, LinkText, and PartialLinkText via the Locator property. When OnElement is absent, element targeting is skipped and coordinates are used instead.

description: ### Purpose
MoveMouseCursor moves the mouse cursor to a target web element or to a specific coordinate position on the page.
When an element is specified via OnElement it takes priority and the cursor moves directly to that element.

When no element is provided, X and Y coordinates define the target position and the optional Origin parameter controls whether the movement is relative to the current pointer position or to the viewport's top-left corner.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature              | Description                                                                                                                                       |
| Element Targeting    | Moves the cursor to a specified web element using any supported locator strategy.                                                                 |
| Coordinate Targeting | Moves the cursor to explicit X and Y coordinates when no element is provided.                                                                     |
| Origin Control       | Accepts 'Pointer' (relative to current cursor position) or 'Viewport' (relative to the viewport top-left corner) as the movement reference point. |
| Locator Flexibility  | Supports Xpath, CssSelector, Id, LinkText, and PartialLinkText locator strategies for element targeting.                                          |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case              | Description                                                                                             |
| Hover Interaction     | Position the cursor over an element to trigger hover states, tooltips, or dropdown menus in a workflow. |
| Mouse Pre-Positioning | Move the cursor to a known coordinate before a scripted click or drag action.                           |
| Tooltip Data Access   | Hover over elements to reveal hidden data or tooltip content for downstream extraction.                 |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case                    | Description                                                                                                  |
| Hover State Testing         | Verify that hover-triggered elements such as tooltips, dropdowns, or overlays appear correctly.              |
| Coordinate Movement Testing | Validate that cursor movement to absolute or relative coordinates behaves as expected.                       |
| Origin Mode Testing         | Confirm that Pointer-relative and Viewport-relative movements produce distinct and correct cursor positions. |
| Element Targeting Testing   | Ensure that element-based cursor moves correctly locate and reach off-screen or nested elements.             |

edge_cases: Element is not in the viewport — the action scrolls it into view before moving the cursor
Element becomes stale between location and move — a StaleElementReferenceException is raised
Element is obscured by another element — the cursor lands on the overlapping element instead
Element is inside an iframe — the driver must be switched to the correct frame before this action
Element does not trigger a hover state — the browser may suppress hover events for non-interactive elements
Tooltip or overlay appears with a delay — a wait action may be required after the move before reading tooltip content
Element has display:none or visibility:hidden — the action may throw because the element is not interactable
annotations.expected_result: The mouse cursor moves to the element matched by the CSS selector #TooltipTarget and any hover state is activated.
annotations.notes: When OnElement is provided, element targeting takes precedence over any coordinate arguments. The cursor is moved directly to the element without using X, Y, or Origin.

description: ### Move the cursor to a web element using a CSS selector
Locates the element matching `#TooltipTarget` using the CssSelector strategy and moves the mouse cursor to it.
Use this form to hover over a specific element to trigger hover states, reveal tooltips, or prepare for a follow-on click or drag action.

edge_cases: X or Y is not specified — defaults to 0 for the missing axis
Coordinates are outside the visible viewport — the cursor moves to the specified position but may not be visible on screen
No prior cursor movement has occurred — cursor starts at a browser-defined default position before this action
Browser zoom level differs from 100% — coordinate mapping may not align with the expected screen position
Page layout shifts after coordinates were calculated — the target area may no longer match the intended position
annotations.expected_result: The mouse cursor moves to the offset (150, 250) relative to its current position.
annotations.notes: When no OnElement is provided the action reads X, Y, and Origin from the argument. Origin defaults to 'pointer' when not specified, making the movement relative to the current cursor position.

description: ### Move the cursor to explicit coordinates
Moves the mouse cursor by (150, 250) relative to the current pointer position (the default Origin).
Use this form to position the cursor at a known offset from its current location before a follow-on interaction.

edge_cases: Viewport dimensions change between the coordinate calculation and this action — the target area may shift
Page is scrolled after coordinates were captured — Viewport origin is always relative to the visible viewport, not the document top
Browser zoom level differs from 100% — coordinate mapping may not align with the expected screen position
Coordinates resolve to a position outside the viewport — the cursor moves there but may not interact with visible content
Coordinates (0, 0) with Viewport origin — cursor moves to the top-left corner of the visible viewport
annotations.expected_result: The mouse cursor moves to coordinates (400, 300) measured from the top-left corner of the visible viewport.
annotations.notes: Setting Origin to 'Viewport' anchors the movement to the viewport's top-left corner regardless of the current cursor position. Use this when target coordinates are calculated relative to the viewport.

description: ### Move the cursor to viewport-relative coordinates
Moves the mouse cursor to coordinates (400, 300) measured from the top-left corner of the visible viewport by setting Origin to 'Viewport'.
Use this form when target coordinates are calculated relative to the viewport rather than the current cursor position.
Specifies the origin reference point for the coordinate-based mouse movement.
Accepted values are 'Pointer' (relative to the current cursor position) and 'Viewport' (relative to the top-left corner of the visible viewport).
Ignored when OnElement is specified.
parameters.name: Origin

description: Movement is relative to the current position of the mouse pointer.
values.name: Pointer

description: Movement is relative to the top-left corner of the visible viewport.
values.name: Viewport

description: Specifies the horizontal pixel offset for the cursor movement.
Defaults to 0 when not provided.
Ignored when OnElement is specified.
parameters.name: X

description: Specifies the vertical pixel offset for the cursor movement.
Defaults to 0 when not provided.
Ignored when OnElement is specified.
parameters.name: Y

description: Argument provides the CLI-formatted parameter string for coordinate-based cursor movements.
Accepted parameters are --X, --Y, and --Origin.
Argument is only evaluated when OnElement is absent; it is ignored when an element is targeted.
properties.name: Argument

description: Locator specifies the strategy used to find the target element for the cursor move.
Accepted values include Xpath, CssSelector, Id, LinkText, and PartialLinkText.
When absent the default Xpath strategy is used.
Locator is only evaluated when OnElement is also provided.
properties.name: Locator

description: OnElement provides the locator expression that identifies the element to move the cursor to.
It is evaluated using the strategy defined by the Locator property.
When provided, element targeting takes precedence over X, Y, and Origin.
properties.name: OnElement

summary: MoveMouseCursor moves the mouse cursor to a target web element or to a specific coordinate position on the page, making it the primary way to position the mouse before hover, click, or drag interactions in an automation workflow.
When an element is provided via OnElement, element targeting takes precedence and the cursor moves directly to that element.

When no element is specified, X and Y coordinates define the target position and the optional Origin parameter controls whether the movement is relative to the current pointer position ('Pointer') or to the viewport's top-left corner ('Viewport').
